Senator Wayne Langerholc, Jr.
Meet Wayne Langerholc, Jr.
Sen. Wayne Langerholc, Jr. was first elected to the Pennsylvania Senate in November 2016 and is serving his third term. His district includes all of Cambria and Clearfield Counties and the western portion of Centre County. He serves in the leadership position of Senate Majority Whip. He is a resident of Cambria County with his wife, Cortney, and their three daughters.
Learn More

ai_summary.summary: Pennsylvania State Senator Wayne Langerholc (R-35) voted in favor of House Bill 1667, a tax cut package that reduces taxes on electric bills and school supplies, expands an education tax credit program, and removes a sales tax exemption for data centers. He also co-sponsored legislation reforming second-degree murder sentencing in response to recent court rulings. Both measures passed the Pennsylvania Senate.

June 25, 2026
Senate Passes Baker, Langerholc Bill to Preserve Accountability in Second-Degree Murder Cases
Sen. Lisa Baker (R-20), chair of the Senate Judiciary Committee, along with Sen. Wayne Langerholc (R-35) and Pennsylvania Attorney General David Sunday announced the Senate’s approval of their legislation updating Pennsylvania’s sentencing laws for second-degree murder in response to a recent court decision. Under Pennsylvania law, second-degree murder — also known as felony murder — applies when a death occurs during the commission of certain felonies, even if the defendant did not directly cause the killing.

Wayne Langerholc (R-35) announced Senate Judiciary Committee passage of legislation they introduced to update Pennsylvania’s sentencing laws for second-degree murder in response to a recent court decision declaring the mandatory life-without-parole sentence for that crime unconstitutional.

ai_summary.summary: Pennsylvania State Senator Wayne Langerholc (R-35) represents the 35th Senate District covering Cambria, Centre, and Clearfield counties. His recent press releases cover votes he participated in on tax legislation, second-degree murder sentencing reform, and law enforcement hunting license discounts, as well as committee actions on human trafficking identification for juvenile offenders. He also announced transportation and infrastructure funding for his district and hosted local constituent events.

Search
If you have questions about Pennsylvania state government or wish to share your thoughts on any matter, please feel free to contact me.
